body.probe: Igor inserts an INTERPRETIVE node during a live turn (no CC intervention), node appears in memory_search next turn
body.title: Inline graph-write: store_memory + add_child + embed_node in turn context
body.result: Created wild_igor/igor/tools/graph_write.py with three registered tools: store_memory (creates a Memory node with source=self_edit, optional parent attachment, valence/arousal, returns new ID), link_memory (adds parent→child edge between existing nodes), embed_node (triggers embedding computation for Phase 2 cosine search, graceful fallback when Ollama unavailable). Safety: per-process rate gate via IGOR_GRAPH_WRITE_LIMIT (default 5). Registered in tools/__init__.py. 14 tests in tests/test_graph_write.py. Commit a746070. Probe (Igor inserts INTERPRETIVE node live) requires Igor running — deferred to next live session.
body.description: Wire store_memory(), add_child(), and embed_node() as callable tools within Igor's inline turn context (think phase). Needed for: Design B autonomous INTERPRETIVE deposit (CONDITION_GATE during reasoning), mid-turn DISTILLATION, and closing the self-modification loop without CC bridge. Current state: Igor can design and verbalize correct habit insertions but cannot execute them — requires CC to bridge every DB write. This is the capability gap that forces the LLM-as-trainer arc to remain open. L-size — requires plan approval.
